chablis.source: AOC Chablis regulations (all levels: Petit Chablis through Grand Cru)

hautes cotes de beaune blanc.source: AOC Bourgogne Hautes Cotes de Beaune regulations (white)

clos vougeot.source: AOC Clos de Vougeot Grand Cru regulations

clos de vougeot.source: AOC Clos de Vougeot Grand Cru regulations

bourgogne hautes cotes de beaune.source: AOC Bourgogne Hautes Cotes de Beaune regulations (red)

hautes cotes de beaune rouge.source: AOC Bourgogne Hautes Cotes de Beaune regulations (red)

sancerre.notes: White context. Red/Rose Sancerre uses Pinot Noir.

chinon.notes: Red Chinon. White Chinon is Chenin Blanc but rare.

saumur champigny.notes: Minor Cabernet Sauvignon permitted up to 15%

saumur-champigny.notes: Minor Cabernet Sauvignon permitted up to 15%

bourgueil.notes: Minor Cabernet Sauvignon permitted up to 10%

pessac leognan.notes: Red Pessac-Leognan. See pessac leognan blanc for white.

pessac-leognan.notes: Red Pessac-Leognan. See pessac leognan blanc for white.

barsac.notes: Botrytis-affected sweet wine. May also label as Sauternes.

hermitage.notes: Red context. Up to 15% Marsanne/Roussanne permitted but rarely used. White Hermitage is Marsanne/Roussanne.

chateauneuf du pape.notes: Red. 13 (now 18) permitted varieties but most blends led by Grenache.

chateauneuf-du-pape.notes: Red. 13 (now 18) permitted varieties but most blends led by Grenache.

cotes du rhone.dominance: varies (often Grenache dominant in Southern Rhone blends)

brunello di montalcino.notes: Locally called Brunello, a clone of Sangiovese Grosso

chianti classico.notes: Up to 20% other authorized red varieties (Canaiolo, Colorino, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah)

recioto della valpolicella.notes: Sweet dried grape wine from Valpolicella

soave.notes: Trebbiano di Soave and Chardonnay permitted up to 30%

soave classico.notes: Trebbiano di Soave and Chardonnay permitted up to 30%

etna rosso.notes: Up to 20% Nerello Cappuccio and other authorized varieties

etna bianco.notes: Catarratto and other white varieties permitted up to 40%

vin santo del chianti.notes: Dried grape dessert wine. Some producers use Sangiovese for Occhio di Pernice style.
vin santo del chianti.source: DOC Vin Santo del Chianti regulations

vin santo del chianti classico.notes: Dried grape dessert wine from Chianti Classico zone
vin santo del chianti classico.source: DOC Vin Santo del Chianti Classico regulations

vin santo del chianti rufina.notes: Dried grape dessert wine from Chianti Rufina zone
vin santo del chianti rufina.source: DOC Vin Santo del Chianti Rufina regulations

vin santo.notes: General Vin Santo. Specific sub-zones have own DOC.

prosecco superiore.notes: DOCG level from Conegliano-Valdobbiadene or Asolo

lambrusco.notes: Multiple sub-varieties: Lambrusco di Sorbara, Grasparossa, Salamino, etc.

franciacorta.notes: Traditional method sparkling. Erbamat also now permitted.

brachetto d'acqui.notes: Sweet, low-alcohol, lightly sparkling red wine

rioja.notes: Red context. Some modern Riojas use international varieties.

rioja blanco.notes: Modern styles may include Tempranillo Blanco, Maturana Blanca, Verdejo

cava.notes: Chardonnay and Pinot Noir also now permitted. Traditional method sparkling.

manzanilla.notes: Fino-style sherry from Sanlucar de Barrameda. Biologically aged under flor.

oloroso.notes: Oxidatively aged. Naturally dry, may be sweetened for commercial styles.

palo cortado.notes: Rare style combining fino nose with oloroso body

amontillado.notes: Initially biologically aged, then oxidatively aged

pedro ximenez.notes: Extremely sweet dessert sherry from sun-dried grapes

jerez.notes: Generic sherry designation. Pedro Ximenez and Moscatel for sweet styles.

port.dominance: varies (field blends traditional; Touriga Nacional most prized)
port.notes: All styles: Vintage/Vintage Port, Ruby, Tawny, LBV, Colheita, White

tawny port.notes: Barrel-aged. Indicated age (10/20/30/40 year) or Colheita.

madeira.notes: If labeled by noble variety (Sercial/Verdelho/Boal/Malmsey), must be minimum 85% that variety. Otherwise typically Tinta Negra.

madeira boal.notes: Medium-sweet style of Madeira. Also spelled Bual.

madeira malmsey.notes: Sweetest style of Madeira. Also known as Malvasia.

douro.notes: Unfortified table wine from the Douro Valley

mateus.notes: Commercial brand rose wine. Not an appellation but a brand name.

blanc de blancs.notes: Champagne context. Made exclusively from white grapes (Chardonnay).

blanc de noirs.notes: Champagne context. White wine made from black grapes only.

cremant de bourgogne.notes: Traditional method sparkling from Burgundy. Gamay also permitted.

cremant de loire.notes: Traditional method sparkling from the Loire Valley

bandol.notes: Red context. Bandol rose uses same varieties but Mourvedre can be lower.

provence rose.notes: Tibouren, Carignan, and Cabernet Sauvignon also permitted

cotes de provence.dominance: varies (often Grenache/Cinsault led for rose; Syrah/Mourvedre for red)

cahors.notes: Locally known as Cot or Auxerrois. Merlot and Tannat as blending partners.

jurancon.notes: Jurancon Sec is dry; Jurancon (without Sec) is sweet/late harvest. Courbu also permitted.

vin jaune.notes: Aged under a veil of flor yeast for minimum 6 years and 3 months in barrel
vin jaune.source: AOC Vin Jaune regulations (Arbois, Chateau-Chalon, L'Etoile, Cotes du Jura)

arbois savagnin.notes: Can be made in oxidative (sous voile) or ouille (topped up) style

arbois.notes: White Arbois can be Savagnin, Chardonnay, or blend. Red Arbois uses Poulsard, Trousseau, Pinot Noir.

l'etoile.notes: White wines only. Can be still, sparkling, or Vin Jaune style.

muscat de beaumes de venise.notes: Vin Doux Naturel. Both white and pink Muscat permitted.

sekt.notes: If Rheingau/Mosel origin, typically Riesling. Generic Sekt may use various varieties. Winzersekt must be estate-grown.
sekt.source: German wine law (Sekt b.A. and Winzersekt regulations)

tokaji aszu.notes: Botrytis-affected sweet wine. Puttonyos indicates sweetness level.

tokaji szamorodni.notes: Made from whole bunches including botrytised berries. Can be sweet (edes) or dry (szaraz).

tokaji.notes: Generic Tokaji designation. Dry Furmint increasingly important.

english sparkling.notes: Traditional method sparkling. Same three Champagne varieties dominant.
english sparkling.source: PDO English Sparkling Wine / WineGB guidelines

rutherglen muscat.notes: Also known as Brown Muscat. Fortified sweet wine classified as Rutherglen/Classic/Grand/Rare.

rubicon, meerlust.notes: Stellenbosch Bordeaux-style blend. Flagship wine of Meerlust Estate.

lytton springs, ridge.notes: Historic field blend. Proportions vary by vintage.

cullen amber.notes: Skin-contact orange wine. Extended maceration on skins.

dom perignon.notes: Vintage Champagne. Moet & Chandon prestige cuvee.

vecchio samperi, marco de bartoli.notes: Unfortified perpetual solera wine. Marsala-adjacent style without fortification.

la bruja, comando g.notes: Old-vine Garnacha from high-altitude granitic soils

villanyi franc.source: Villany PDO regulations; Franc = Cabernet Franc in Hungarian context

toscana igt.notes: Catch-all for Super Tuscan wines; actual blend varies by producer

quarts de chaume.source: AOC Quarts de Chaume Grand Cru (sweet Chenin Blanc)

coteaux du layon.source: AOC Coteaux du Layon (sweet Chenin Blanc)

priorat.dominance: old-vine Garnacha + Carinena, often blended with Cabernet/Syrah

penedes.notes: For sparkling; still Penedes varieties vary

graves.notes: Graves Blanc = Sauvignon Blanc/Semillon; Graves Rouge = Bordeaux blend

anjou.notes: Anjou Blanc = Chenin; Anjou Rouge = Cabernet Franc

mercurey.notes: Cote Chalonnaise; predominantly red Pinot Noir

bourgogne cote chalonnaise.notes: Regional Burgundy from the Cote Chalonnaise

etna.notes: Etna Rosso = Nerello Mascalese; Etna Bianco = Carricante
